title: chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. variabile
intro: Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. variabile, also known as yellow buttons, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. variabile include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve skin conditions, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or oils, which can be used to treat various health conditions, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
